en: Oyster shell (Muli). Taste salty, neutral. Indicated for cold-damage fever and chills, shivering in warm-type malaria, and fright and anger qi. Relieves muscle spasm and flaccidity, scrofula, and women's red and white vaginal discharge. Taken long-term, strengthens the bones and joints, wards off evil spirits, and prolongs life. Also called Ligé. Grows in ponds and marshes.

Carp gallbladder. Taste bitter, cold. Indicated for red, painful eyes from heat, and blue-blindness; brightens the eyes. Taken long-term, strengthens vigor and boosts the will and vital energy. Grows in ponds and marshes.
Snakehead fish. Taste sweet, cold. Indicated for damp bi-syndrome with facial and eye swelling; drains excess water. Also called Tong fish. Grows in ponds and marshes.

Rhinoceros horn. Taste bitter, cold. Indicated for the hundred poisons and gu-toxin infestations, evil spirits and miasmic qi; neutralizes the poisons of gouwen (Gelsemium), toxic bird feathers, and snake venom. Dispels evil influences and prevents confusion and nightmares. Taken long-term, lightens the body. Grows in river valleys.

Antelope horn. Taste salty, cold. Brightens the eyes, boosts qi, and revives sexual vigor. Removes malign blood flowing downward, wards off gu-toxin, evil spirits, and inauspicious influences, calms the heart-qi, and prevents nightmares. Taken long-term, strengthens the sinews and bones and lightens the body. Grows in river valleys.

Black goat horn. Taste salty, warm. Indicated for blue-blindness; brightens the eyes, kills scabies mites, stops cold-type diarrhea, wards off evil spirits, tigers and wolves, and stops fright palpitations. Taken long-term, calms the heart, boosts qi, and lightens the body. Grows in river valleys.

White horse penis. Taste salty, neutral. Indicated for internal injury with the pulse cut off, and impotence. Strengthens the will, boosts qi, builds muscle, and promotes vigor and fertility. The eye treats fright epilepsy, abdominal fullness, and malaria. The dew-claw treats fright-evil convulsions, difficult labor, and wards off malign qi, ghosts, poison, and gu-toxin infestations. Grows in level marshes.

Dog penis. Taste salty, neutral. Indicated for internal injury and impotence; makes one strong and virile and aids conception, and removes the twelve ailments of women's vaginal discharge. The gallbladder brightens the eyes. Also called Dog Essence. Grows in level marshes.

Deer antler velvet. Taste sweet, warm. Indicated for vaginal spotting with malign blood, chills and fever, and fright epilepsy; boosts qi, strengthens the will, and promotes tooth growth and longevity. The antler (once hardened) treats malignant sores and welling-abscesses, expels evil and malign qi, and clears stagnant blood retained in the vagina.

Bat. Taste salty, neutral. Indicated for dim vision; brightens the eyes and grants clear night vision. Taken long-term, makes one joyful, charming, and carefree. Also called Bianfu. Grows in the river valleys of Mount Tai.

Hedgehog skin. Taste bitter, neutral. Indicated for the five types of hemorrhoids with genital erosion, bleeding with red and white discharge, unceasing multicolored bloody fluid, and genital swelling and pain radiating to the lower back. Kill the hedgehog by boiling it in wine before use. Grows in river valleys.

Skink. Taste salty, cold. Indicated for the five types of urinary blockage and bound evil qi; breaks up stone strangury, stops bleeding, and promotes urination. Also called Xiyi (lizard). Grows in river valleys.

Mantis egg case (Sangpiaoxiao). Taste salty, neutral. Indicated for internal injury, hernia-like masses, and impotence; boosts essence and aids conception. Treats women's amenorrhea with lower back pain, relieves the five types of painful urinary dysfunction, and promotes urination. Grows on mulberry branches; gather and steam before use. Also called Shiyou.
Cicada. Taste salty, cold. Indicated for infant fright epilepsy, night crying, epilepsy, and chills and fever. Grows on willow trees.

White stiff silkworm. Taste salty. Indicated for infant fright epilepsy and night crying; removes the three parasites, clears dark facial blemishes, improves the complexion, and treats genital sores in men. Grows in level marshes.

Wood horsefly. Taste bitter, neutral. Indicated for red, painful eyes, injured canthus with tearing, stagnant blood and amenorrhea, chills and fever with aching, and infertility. Also called Huncháng. Grows in river marshes.

Flying horsefly (Feimeng). Taste bitter, slightly cold. Expels stagnant blood, breaks down hardened blood accumulations, masses and concretions with chills and fever, and frees the blood vessels and the nine orifices. Grows in river valleys.

Feilian (a beetle-like insect). Taste salty. Indicated for blood stasis and hardened masses with chills and fever; breaks up accumulations, treats throat bi-obstruction, and internal cold with infertility. Grows in river marshes.

White grub (dung beetle larva). Taste salty, slightly warm. Indicated for malign blood and blood stasis, bi-syndrome qi; breaks up bruised blood causing hardness, fullness, and pain under the ribs, amenorrhea, and excess tissue growth in the eye with bluish nebula and white membrane. Also called Fenqi. Grows in level marshes.

Slug. Taste salty, cold. Indicated for pathogenic wind causing facial paralysis, tendon injury and rectal prolapse, and fright epilepsy with spasmodic contraction. Also called Lingli. Grows in ponds and marshes.

Sea clam. Taste bitter, neutral. Indicated for cough with rising qi, wheezing with vexation and fullness, and chest pain with chills and fever. Also called Kuige. Grows in ponds and marshes. Wenge (patterned clam) treats malignant sores and erodes the five types of hemorrhoids.

Turtle shell. Taste salty, neutral. Indicated for vaginal spotting with red and white discharge; breaks up masses and concretions, treats chronic malaria, the five types of hemorrhoids with genital erosion, damp bi-syndrome, heaviness and weakness of the limbs, and unclosed fontanelle in infants. Taken long-term, lightens the body and suppresses hunger. Also called Spirit House. Grows in ponds and marshes.

Soft-shell turtle shell. Taste salty, neutral. Indicated for masses and concretions in the chest and abdomen, hardened accumulations with chills and fever; removes epigastric fullness, polyps, genital erosion, hemorrhoids, and malignant flesh growths. Grows in ponds and marshes.

Tuo-fish scale armor (armored fish scales). Taste pungent, slightly warm. Indicated for masses and concretions in the chest and abdomen, latent hardened accumulations, chills and fever, women's flooding with multicolored uterine bleeding, pain linking the lower abdomen and genitals, scabies sores, and necrotic flesh. Grows in ponds and marshes.

Cuttlefish bone. Taste salty, slightly warm. Indicated for women's spotting with red and white menstrual discharge, amenorrhea, genital erosion with swelling and pain, chills and fever, masses and concretions, and infertility. Grows in ponds and marshes.

Crab. Taste salty, cold. Indicated for evil qi in the chest, painful heat binding, and facial paralysis with swelling. Counteracts the toxicity of lacquer. When burned, it draws out rats. Grows in ponds and marshes.
